pr.title: Matched if_2F72 (if_802F7E7C) by converting the while to a for loop
pr.author: DavidWeaverAudio

summary: PR 2570 matched src/melee/if/if_2F72.c function if_802F7E7C by replacing a do-while loop that advanced a secondary data pointer with a for loop indexed over the original base pointer. The bot report records one new match for main/melee/if/if_2F72 if_802F7E7C, reaching 100.00% for that item.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; updates if_802F7E7C loop structure and array access form.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/if/if_2F72.c added 8 deleted 10 hunks 1
raw_diff: if_802F7E7C hunk
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report for GALE01

what_changed.text: Removed the separate HSD_GObj** data cursor initialized from lbl_804A1340 and kept a single base pointer for later use by memzero.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed HSD_GObj** data = (HSD_GObj**) lbl_804A1340; kept HSD_GObj** base = (HSD_GObj**) lbl_804A1340

what_changed.text: Changed a do-while loop with manual i++ and data += 2 advancement into for (i = 0; i < 6; i++).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: replaced do { ... i++; data += 2; } while (i < 6); with for (i = 0; i < 6; i++)

what_changed.text: Changed object checks from cursor-relative data[1] and data[2] to base-indexed base[i * 2 + 1] and base[i * 2 + 2], preserving calls to HSD_GObjPLink_80390228 for non-null entries.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: data[1]/data[2] replaced with base[i * 2 + 1]/base[i * 2 + 2]

what_changed.text: The bot report records one new match: main/melee/if/if_2F72 item if_802F7E7C, +9 bytes, from 93.12% to 100.00%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: issue_comment by decomp-dev[bot], Report for GALE01, 1 new match

smart_moves.text: Kept the original base pointer available for the final memzero(base, 0x34) while expressing repeated object cleanup as indexed accesses.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: base used in loop expressions and memzero(base, 0x34)

smart_moves.text: Used a natural counted for loop for six repeated two-slot groups instead of retaining a generated-looking pointer-advance do-while.
evidence_refs: PR title: converting the while to a for loop
raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 6; i++) with base[i * 2 + 1] and base[i * 2 + 2]

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a loop walks fixed-size repeated entries but later code still needs the original base address, try a counted for loop over the base pointer rather than a separate advancing cursor.
scope: functions that reuse the base pointer after iteration
evidence_refs: raw_diff: data cursor removed; base retained for indexed loop and memzero
text_corpus: bot report records if_802F7E7C reached 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: A do-while with manual counter increment and pointer stride can be a decompiler-shaped form; rewriting it as for (i = 0; i < bound; i++) may both improve readability and match.
evidence_refs: PR title: Matched if_2F72 (if_802F7E7C) by converting the while to a for loop
raw_diff: do-while replaced by for loop
text_corpus: 1 new match for if_802F7E7C

naming_conventions.text: The PR uses the local role name base for the persistent pointer to lbl_804A1340 and removes the extra cursor name data.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed data declaration; retained HSD_GObj** base

naming_conventions.text: No new semantic global names were introduced; the existing address-style lbl_804A1340 and function name if_802F7E7C were preserved.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: references to lbl_804A1340 and if_802F7E7C remain

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Try converting pointer-advance loops into indexed for loops when the stride is regular and the original base pointer remains live after the loop.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: data += 2 removed; indexed expressions base[i * 2 + 1] and base[i * 2 + 2] added
text_corpus: bot report shows if_802F7E7C reached 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use exact integer bounds visible in the generated loop, here i < 6, when testing natural loop forms for matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: while (i < 6) became for (i = 0; i < 6; i++)

follow_up_queries: do while data += 2 for loop match

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use indexed for loops for regular pointer-stride cleanup when the base pointer remains needed
accepted_candidate_records.text: In if_802F7E7C, replacing a do-while that advanced a separate HSD_GObj** cursor by two entries with a counted for loop over base[i * 2 + 1] and base[i * 2 + 2] preserved the base pointer for memzero(base, 0x34) and corresponded to a reported new 100.00% match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: if_802F7E7C hunk
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report, main/melee/if/if_2F72 if_802F7E7C 93.12% to 100.00%

rejection_notes: Do not generalize this PR into a rule that all do-while loops should become for loops; the evidence is limited to a fixed-bound, regular-stride loop in if_802F7E7C.
No source-owned path fact or global naming standard is proposed because the PR only changes one function and includes no review discussion.
